components.role: Filter/coupling capacitor — blocks DC and low frequencies below cutoff
Filter resistor — provides DC path to ground and sets cutoff with C1

spice_template:
C1 in out {{C1}}
R1 out gnd {{R1}}

dsp_model.description: First-order IIR highpass filter; -3dB at cutoff, -20dB/decade rolloff below

behavioral_description: A first-order highpass filter that removes low-frequency content below the cutoff. This is the fundamental bass-cut building block used everywhere in pedal circuits: before clipping stages to prevent muddy distortion, as coupling capacitors between stages, and as explicit bass-cut controls. At a cutoff of 100-200Hz, it tightens the low end and prevents boomy distortion. At higher cutoffs (500Hz+), it thins out the sound dramatically, useful for treble-boost effects or cocked-wah tones. Every coupling capacitor in a pedal circuit is effectively a highpass filter, so understanding this block is essential for voicing a pedal's overall bass response.
